CG Week 2026
Tue June 2 – Fri June 5
Rutgers University
College Avenue Campus
New Brunswick, NJ, USA

    About

    The Computational Geometry Week (CG Week) is the premier international forum for advances in Computational Geometry and its applications. CG Week combines a number of events, most notably the International Symposium on Computational Geometry (SoCG), the associated CG Media Exposition (CG:ME), the Young Researchers Forum (CG:YRF), the CG:Challenge, and various workshops.

    News

    • April 17, 2026: The preliminary at-a-glance program is now available.
    • February 26, 2026: Registration for CG Week 2026 is now open.
    • February 24, 2026: The list of accepted SoCG papers can be found here.
    • February 5, 2026: Local information is now available under the Attend section.
    • October 3, 2025: The SoCG call for papers can now be found here.
    • July 16, 2025: Website created.

    Code of conduct

    CG Week is dedicated to providing an environment that is free from harassment, bullying, discrimination, and retaliation for all participants. Starting in 2025, CG Week will be organized as an event of the CG Society. All members of the Society are bound by its Code of Conduct. Only members of the Society can give a presentation and hence at least one author of each accepted paper must become a member of the Society. Society membership is free.

    Three SafeToC advocates, Erin Chambers, Jeff Erickson, and Emily Fox, will be attending CG Week 2026. Feel free to reach out to them if you have any concerns.

    Local organizers

    • Jie Gao (Computer Science, Rutgers)
    • David Pennock (DIMACS, Rutgers)
    • Tamra Carpenter (DIMACS, Rutgers)
